input.description: Regenerate route tree and verify build

content.text:
Here’s what was implemented.

### `DetailPanelShell` (`detail-panel-chrome.tsx`)

- Exported **`DetailPanelEditAction`** (`onClick`, optional `disabled`).
- Optional **`editAction`** renders a **`Pencil`** `IconButton` with **`aria-label="Edit"`**.
- **Primary (left) chrome** is a **`flex` row**: optional “open full page”, then optional edit (same pattern as before for full-page visibility via `useMatchRoute`).
- **Secondary (right)** stays the close control when `showCloseButton` is true.

### Plot detail (`plot-detail-panel.tsx`)

- **`openPlotEdit`** calls `openPanel(PLOT_EDIT_PANEL_TYPE, { plotId })`.
- **`plotEditChrome`** is set when **`canManageData`** and **`plotId`** are defined (loading / error / missing plot / main view).
- The body **“Edit raw metrics”** button still calls **`openPlotEdit`** so behaviour matches the chrome control.

### Profile detail (`profile-detail-panel.tsx`)

- **`shellChromeProps`** is built **after** **`openProfileEdit`** and **`canManageData`** so it can include **`editAction`** when **`profileId`** is set and the user can manage data (panel **and** **`surface="page"`**).
- The existing **“Edit weights”** section still uses **`openProfileEdit`**.

### Profile full-page route

- Added **`plots.profiles.$profileId.tsx`** so **`/plots/profiles/$profileId`** is registered and **`matchRoute` / `navigate`** typings stay correct for the profile full-page target.

**Note:** **Criterion detail** has no **`criterion-edit`** entry in `panel-registry`, so no pencil was added there. If you add that panel later, you can pass **`editAction`** the same way.

Build completes successfully with the updated route tree.

content.text:
Update is confined to `DetailPanelShell` in `detail-panel-chrome.tsx`.

**CoverView wiring:** `primaryAction` is **left** (`pl-4`), `secondaryAction` is **right** (`pr-4`). So now:

- **`primaryAction`** → **close** (`DetailPanelCloseButton`) when `showCloseButton` is true  
- **`secondaryAction`** → a **`flex justify-end gap-1`** group with **open full page** (when applicable) and **edit** (when applicable)

Order on the right stays **full page → pencil**, grouped on the right. No changes were required inside `CoverView.tsx`; only how the app passes props into it.

input.description: Verify production build after CoverView and ui-lib changes

content.text:
Here’s what was implemented:

### Shared tier + tint (`score-badge.tsx`)
- **`scoreTierFromOutOfFive(score)`** — same bands as criterion badges: **≥ 4 → high**, **≥ 3 → mid**, else **low** (works with fractional “x.xx / 5” profile scores).
- **`scoreTierCoverHeaderTintClass`** — **`bg-score-{high|mid|low}/35`** plus a light **border-b**, so the cover header reads like the pills (`/15`) but a bit stronger.

`PlotCriterionEditorCard` now uses **`scoreTierFromOutOfFive`** instead of a duplicate helper.

### `CoverView` (`CoverView.tsx`)
- New optional **`headerClassName`**, merged onto the collapsing header (`cn` + tailwind-merge so it can override the default `colorScheme` background).

### `DetailPanelShell` (`detail-panel-chrome.tsx`)
- New optional **`coverHeaderClassName`** → passed through as **`headerClassName`** on `CoverView`.

### Plot detail panel (`plot-detail-panel.tsx`)
- Finds **Private use** by **`code === 'PRIVATE_USE'`** (case-insensitive) or **`id === 'private_use'`**.
- Uses **`percentage / 20`** as the **1–5** score, derives **`scoreTierFromOutOfFive`**, applies **`scoreTierCoverHeaderTintClass[tier]`** as **`coverHeaderClassName`** on the loaded plot view only (loading / errors stay default).

Exports were added in **`packages/ui-lib/src/components/presentation/index.ts`** (also available via the `@portugal/ui-lib` barrel).

**Note:** Rankings in **`RankingsTable`** still default tiers from **rank** when `scoreTier` is omitted (dashboard passes rank-based tiers). The plot header tint follows **numeric thresholds**, aligned with **`ScoreBadge`** / criterion scoring, which matches “thresholded range” on the actual score. If you want dashboard pills to use the same numeric rule instead of rank, that can be a small follow-up in `plot-profiles-dashboard-page.tsx` / `profile-detail-panel.tsx`.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what changed:

### `score-badge.tsx` (ui-lib)
- Added **`scoreTierForegroundClass`**: `text-score-high` | `text-score-mid` | `text-score-low` — same tokens as the ranking **`ScoreBadge`** text.
- Split badge styling into **`tierBackgroundClass`** + **`scoreTierForegroundClass`** so pills and cover titles share one source for the tier color.

### `presentation/index.ts`
- Re-export **`scoreTierForegroundClass`**.

### `CoverView`
- Mobile **`h2`** title now appends **`titleProps?.className`** so tier colors apply there too (tailwind-merge prefers it over default `text-foreground`).

### `DetailPanelShell`
- New optional **`coverTitleClassName`** → **`titleProps={{ className }}`** on **`CoverView`**.

### `plot-detail-panel.tsx`
- Computes **`privateUseTier`** once from the private-use score (same **`scoreTierFromOutOfFive`** as the header tint).
- Sets **`plotCoverTitleClassName`** to **`scoreTierForegroundClass[privateUseTier]`** whenever that tier exists.

The main cover **`motion.h1`** already merged **`titleProps.className`** last, so **`text-score-*`** overrides the default **`text-foreground`** there as well.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what was updated.

### `RankingsTable` (`rankings-table.tsx`)
- Added optional **`scoreOutOfFive`** on **`RankingsRowData`** (1–5 scale, same as `percentage / 20`).
- Tier is now  
  **`row.scoreTier ?? scoreTierFromOutOfFive(row.scoreOutOfFive) ?? "mid"`**  
  — rank is **no longer** used for colors.
- **Rank** column uses **`scoreTierForegroundClass[tier]`** so it matches the **ScoreBadge** tier color (replacing “#1 = green, rest muted”).

### Call sites
- **`plot-profiles-dashboard-page.tsx`** — rows pass **`scoreOutOfFive: pct / 20`**; removed rank-based **`scoreTier`**.
- **`profile-detail-panel.tsx`** — same for the top-five table.

### Stories / fixtures
- **`presentation-table.stories.tsx`** — each row includes **`scoreOutOfFive`** aligned with the displayed score (4.92 → high, 3.85 → mid, 2.10 → low).
- **`plot-profile-dashboard-widget.fixtures.tsx`** — replaced **`scoreTier`** with **`scoreOutOfFive`** and adjusted displayed scores so tiers match **`scoreTierFromOutOfFive`** (e.g. 4.25 high, 3.4 mid, 2.9 low, 1.15 low).

Thresholds stay the shared **`scoreTierFromOutOfFive`** rules: **≥ 4 → high**, **≥ 3 → mid**, **< 3 → low**. You can still set **`scoreTier`** on a row when you need an explicit override.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Verify frontend-web build after CoverView API change

content.text:
Here’s the refactor.

### `CoverView` (`CoverView.tsx`)

1. **`coverBackgroundClassName`**  
   - Applied on the **collapsing cover strip** (`motion.div` with `COVER_HEIGHT`), **after** the `colorScheme` surface classes so things like `bg-score-high/35` can override the default fill.

2. **`coverTitleClassName`**  
   - Applied on **both** the desktop **`motion.h1`** and the **mobile toolbar `h2`**, **after** `titleProps.className`, so one prop styles the title everywhere.

3. **`titleDefaultColorClass`**  
   - Single mapping for title color (cover image / primary / secondary / accent → white; card → card foreground; else foreground), used for **h1 and h2** so mobile matches desktop and custom title classes compose predictably.

4. **`headerClassName`**  
   - Still supported but marked **`@deprecated`** in favor of **`coverBackgroundClassName`**.  
   - Both are merged as `cn(headerClassName, coverBackgroundClassName)` so the new prop **wins** on conflicting utilities.

5. **`titleProps`**  
   - Still used for **`id`**, **`style`**, aria, etc. Docs note that **Tailwind-only** title tweaks should prefer **`coverTitleClassName`**.

### `DetailPanelShell` (`detail-panel-chrome.tsx`)

- Renamed **`coverHeaderClassName`** → **`coverBackgroundClassName`** and wired it to **`CoverView`’s `coverBackgroundClassName`**.
- **`coverTitleClassName`** is passed through to **`CoverView`’s `coverTitleClassName`** (no more **`titleProps={{ className }}`** shim).

### Plot detail panel

- Renamed local **`plotCoverHeaderClassName`** → **`plotCoverBackgroundClassName`** and passes **`coverBackgroundClassName`**.

Existing **`CoverView`** stories and other callers keep working; anything still on **`headerClassName`** continues to function until migrated.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Show recent commits on portugal main

content.text:
Here is what the repo shows after following the **git-commits** skill (inspect status, then commit only real, intentional changes).

### Portugal (`/Users/psoudan/workspace/11-personal/portugal`)

**There is nothing to commit:** `git status` reports a clean working tree. Your branch is **ahead of `origin/main` by 4 commits**; recent history is:

| Commit | Summary |
|--------|---------|
| `3718741` | `chore: ignore Storybook static export directory` |
| `c962f0c` | `fix(ui): align management shell, AppProvider, and profile panel` |
| `77f1b33` | `feat(frontend-web): expand plot workspace with dashboards and panels` |
| `90f5b53` | `feat(estate): add obsidian shell, sewdn panels, and UI refresh` |

So the session’s Portugal work is already recorded in those commits. To publish them: `git push` from that repo.

### Other workspace roots
